lenses.guidance: The refrain 'as the LORD commanded Moses' repeats across the chapter (vv 1, 5, 7, 21, 26, 29, 31, 32) — eight beats hammering one note. The repetition is the literary point: obedient pattern-keeping is itself a form of worship.

Aaron's vesting (vv 1-31) anticipates Hebrews 4-10's vesting of the great High Priest. The breastpiece of vv 8-14 carries the twelve names — pattern: the priest carries his people before the Father in the Most Holy place.

v 43 — 'Moses saw all the work, and behold, they had done it... and Moses blessed them.' The text echoes Gen 1:31's 'God saw everything... very good' — tabernacle-completion is narrated as a creation-act, with Moses voicing the divine refrain.
